name: bash
description: Run a shell command in the workspace root. Output is truncated if long; the command is killed on timeout.

background.description: Run the command as a background task: returns a task handle immediately; the result arrives as a message when it finishes.

notify.description: For background tasks only: when the finished task's outcome flows back to you as a message. 'always' (default), 'on_fail' (only when it fails), or 'none' (fire-and-forget: no message, the result is not retrievable — use only for tasks whose outcome you genuinely do not need).
